explanation: The phrase "In PCV and PSV modes" defines a contextual precondition, which translates to the disjunction (PCVMode | PSVMode). This condition globally guards the entire behavior. The statement "When the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI" identifies the trigger for the subsequent actions, mapping to the atomic proposition inspiratoryPauseButton. This forms an implication G((PCVMode | PSVMode) -> (inspiratoryPauseButton -> ...)). The ventilator's action to "wait for the end of the next inspiration phase" means that after the inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed, the event inspiratoryPhaseEnd must occur in the next state, represented by X(inspiratoryPhaseEnd). The additional condition "and if the Inspiratory Pause still required" means that the inspiratoryPause must be true at the exact moment inspiratoryPhaseEnd occurs. These two conditions combine to (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPause). If this combined condition holds, then "both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will close" (inValveClose & outValveClose) and remain closed "until the inspiratory pause is stopped by the GUI" (!inspiratoryPauseButton). The 'until' operator U connects these two parts. The entire sequence is nested within the X operator, as it refers to events happening after the initial inspiratoryPauseButton press. The final LTL formula encapsulates this logic: G((PCVMode | PSVMode) -> (inspiratoryPauseButton -> X(inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPause -> ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton)))).

The 'until' operator (U) is used here, with '!inspiratoryPauseButton' signifying the stop condition. Therefore, the complete LTL formula is G(inspiratoryPauseButton -> F(inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton & ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton))).

explanation: The statement begins by setting a context: 'In PCV and PSV modes', which translates to the disjunction (PCVMode | PSVMode). The trigger for the specific behavior is 'When the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI', represented by inspiratoryPauseButton. These two parts form the antecedent of a global implication: G( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton -> ... ). The phrase 'the ventilator shall wait for the end of the next inspiration phase' implies that the specific action of closing valves related to the pause (!(inValveClose & outValveClose)) will not occur until inspiratoryPhaseEnd occurs. This 'waiting' behavior is captured by an 'Until' operator where the negation of the action !(inValveClose & outValveClose) holds until inspiratoryPhaseEnd. Following this, 'if the Inspiratory Pause still required' sets a conditional check, translated as inspiratoryPause. If this condition holds at the moment inspiratoryPhaseEnd is met, 'both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will close', which is (inValveClose & outValveClose). This state of closed valves 'until the inspiratory pause is stopped by the GUI', meaning !inspiratoryPause becomes true, forms another 'Until' clause: ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPause). Combining these, the overall formula states that globally, if the ventilator is in PCV or PSV mode and the Inspiratory Pause is requested, then the valves will not close due to the pause until the end of the next inspiration phase, at which point (if the pause is still required), the valves will close and remain closed until the pause is no longer required.

The core trigger for the inspiratory pause sequence is 'When the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI', which translates to `inspiratoryPauseButton`.
